name: Shiny for Python
tagline: Build reactive web apps and dashboards in pure Python
seoTitle: Shiny for Python - Reactive Data Apps Framework
metaDescription: Shiny for Python is Posit's reactive framework for building interactive dashboards and data apps in pure Python. Install with pip and scaffold an app via shiny create.
overview: Shiny for Python is a framework from Posit for building interactive web applications in Python. It is the Python port of the R Shiny framework and uses reactive programming, so your UI updates automatically when its underlying inputs and data change. You can stand up a simple interactive visualization or a prototype quickly, then grow the same code into a larger application.

It is aimed at data scientists and Python developers who want to ship dashboards and data tools without writing separate frontend code. Unlike frameworks limited to small scripts, Shiny supports modules so you can structure and scale large, mission-critical apps.

Within the data-app builder category, Shiny sits alongside tools like Dash and Streamlit. Posit positions it around reactive programming and extensibility, with hosting options including Connect Cloud, shinyapps.io, Hugging Face, and the in-browser Shinylive runtime.
features: Reactive programming model so outputs re-run automatically when inputs change
A shiny create CLI that scaffolds a new app from a chosen template
shiny run with --reload and --launch-browser for a live development loop
Modules for organizing and scaling large applications
Both Express and Core API styles for writing apps
Multiple hosting paths: Connect Cloud, shinyapps.io, Hugging Face, and Shinylive (runs in the browser)

gettingStarted.intro: Install Shiny from pip, scaffold an app with the CLI, then run it locally with live reload.

steps.heading: Create your first app
steps.body: Run shiny create and pick a template when the CLI prompts you. This generates an app.py to start from.

steps.heading: Run the app
steps.body: Launch the app with live reload and open it in your browser. You can also run it from the Shiny editor extension.

useCases: Build an interactive dashboard to explore a dataset without writing separate frontend code
Prototype a data tool in an afternoon and grow it into a production application
Share a model or analysis as a hosted web app via shinyapps.io, Connect Cloud, or Hugging Face
Ship a self-contained app that runs entirely in the browser using Shinylive
